The founding First Release of Bardstown Bourbon Company's collaboration with Copper & Kings American Brandy — Kentucky straight bourbon finished in Copper & Kings Muscat wine barrels at two distinct proof points. Copper & Kings is a Louisville-based American brandy distillery known for their non-traditional approach to brandy production — using Muscat grapes from California and aging inâ¦

bottles.desc: Our Discovery Series highlights the Art of Blending, creating exciting expressions that together are greater than the sum of its parts. Through artfully combining rare and exemplary whiskies we discover from all over the world, this series pushes the boundaries of innovation by creating completely unique flavor profiles and styles. Each release gives you something new to discover. TASTING NOTES Toasted Pecan, maple, and baked cherry lead to a luscious palate of vanilla cream, hazelnut, and cocoa. A lasting finish balances deep, decadent flavors with lasting spice.

bottles.desc: Our Discovery Series highlights the Art of Blending, creating exciting expressions that together are greater than the sum of its parts. Through artfully combining rare and exemplary whiskies we discover from all over the world, this series pushes the boundaries of innovation by creating completely unique flavor profiles and styles. TASTING NOTES A vibrant blend of rainier cherry, butterscotch, brown sugar, and roasted peanut leads to a spirited palate of toffee and cinnamon spice. Bardstown Bourbon Company opened in 2016 in Bardstown, Kentucky — the self-proclaimed bourbon capital of the world — with a dual mission: produce their own whiskey from grain to glass, and collaborate with other distilleries on unique blending projects. The Discovery Series is the latter — a collaborative blending program that sources exceptional aged bourbon from partner distilleries and assembles it into a unified expression underâ¦

The Discovery Series is Bardstown's premium sourced-blend program, bringing together well-aged stock to create layered, complex bourbons. Discovery #3 marries its three components into a rich, enduring pour. Tasting Notes Nose: Layered maple, a burst of cinnamon, and ripe pear over aged leather Palate: Tannic oak embedded in rich flavor, warm and full Finish: Remarkable and enduring Specs Distillery: Bardstown Bourbon Company,â¦
bottles.name: Bardstown Bourbon Company Discovery Series #3

bottles.desc: Bardstown Bourbon Company Discovery Series #4 is the fourth release in Bardstown’s sourced-and-blended Discovery series — drawing entirely from 10+ year-old bourbon, with heavy emphasis on 13 and 15 year-old barrels. The mash bill across the source whiskeys is roughly three-quarters corn with moderate rye contribution (13–18%) and malted barley making the balance (no wheat) — producing a structured, spice-forward older bourbon with deep oak integration. Bardstown’s Discovery series spotlights the producer’s blending expertise rather than their own distillation — each release selects, marries,â¦
bottles.name: Bardstown Bourbon Company Discovery Series #4

bottles.desc: The fourth release in the Discovery series is all 10+ year-old bourbon and draws heavily from 13 and 15 year-old barrels. A blend of three-quarters corn makes the mash bill in each of the whiskeys selected for Bardstown's flagship blend, along with a moderate rye contribution at 13-18 percent, with malted barley (no wheat) making the difference.

bottles.desc: The Discovery Series showcases the art of blending hand-selected, supremely aged bourbon. For each release, team members throughout the distillery taste, analyze, and vote on the best recipe. TASTING NOTES Walnut and brown sugar with bursts of white flowers and tangerine create a delightful aroma leading to baked Honeycrisp apple and dark chocolate on the palate. Our first international Discovery delivers smooth complexity with every sip.

bottles.desc: All our Discovery Series releases push the boundaries of whiskey innovation, but Series #8 takes the program to a new level with our most complex creation to date. We start with an artfully crafted blend of Kentucky Bourbon, two Indiana ryes, and aged Canadian whiskey. We then add a custom selection of five oak staves that were created specifically to enhance the unique flavor of our original blend. The staves provide nuances that range from French toast to vanilla to Rioja barrels. The result is a celebration of innovation and a spirit bursting with flavor. TASTING NOTES Maple, darkâ¦

bottles.desc: Our Discovery Series has quickly gained a reputation for showcasing exquisite, innovative blends. Series #9 takes the spirit of discovery further by uncovering rare barrels of craft Georgia bourbon with a unique, elegant flavor. The blend is structured around this distinctive profile, adding classic Kentucky bourbon, 17-yr. Tennessee Whiskey, and a touch of sweetness from Canada. It is a combination of tradition and forward-thinking, large-scale and craft. The elements fold into one another remarkably, creating a robust, vibrant, one-of-a-kind pour. TASTING NOTES A decadent bouquet of cocoaâ¦

bottles.desc: Discover the Art of Blending The Bardstown Bourbon Company Double Barreled Discovery Series #13 is a masterpiece for those who savor the finer things in life. bottles.desc: Fusion Series #1. Bardstown Bourbon Company — the founding release in Bardstown Bourbon Company's Fusion series, blending the distillery's own young production with aged sourced Kentucky bourbon in a format that directly documents the distillery's maturation trajectory. The Fusion concept is BBC's most transparent product: each release blends a portion of the distillery's own-produced bourbon (young, vibrant, grain-forward) with a portion of sourced aged Kentucky bourbon (older, more developed oak and fruit character). As the own-distilled component ages over time, subsequent Fusion batchesâ¦

bottles.desc: Bardstown Bourbon Company Fusion Series #3 blends 60% of Bardstown Bourbon Company's own three-year-old wheated and high-rye Kentucky bourbons with 40% of a 13-year Kentucky bourbon — fusing youthful and aged stock into a smooth, complex pour. The first bourbon accounts for 54% of the blend and features a mash bill of 75% corn, 21% rye, and 4% malted barley. The second is crafted with 60% corn and 40% rye, while the third has a 4-grain recipe of 60% corn, 26% rye, 10% wheat, and 4% malted barley. The remaining 30% of the components are distributed between 2 sourced Kentucky whiskeys, both 12 years of age. Bottled at 98.1â¦

Taylor Bottled-in-Bond — the most current annual edition of the expression that honors the man who lobbied for the federal standard it exemplifies. Colonel Edmund Haynes Taylor Jr. championed the Bottled-in-Bond Act of 1897 — the first federal consumer protection law in American spirits history. The BiB standard requires single distillery, single distilling season, minimum four years aging, exactly 100 proof, and federally bonded government-supervised warehouse production. Every E.H. Taylor Bottled-in-Bond is a direct tribute to thatâ¦
bottles.name: Colonel E.H. Taylor Bottled-In-Bond Straight Bourbon 2025

bottles.desc: Colonel E.H. Taylor Cured Oak Bourbon 2015 Release is an experimental release from the Colonel E.H. Taylor collection at Buffalo Trace — aged in barrels constructed from oak that was cured for an extended period before barrel construction, producing a uniquely refined and complex flavor profile. Bottled in Bond, a designation that guarantees age and proof to protect consumers from whiskey of dubious origin. Colonel E.H. Taylor, Jr. was the visionary 19th-century distiller credited as the “Father of the Modern Bourbon Industry” — he pioneered the use of copper fermentation tanks,â¦
bottles.name: Colonel E.H. Taylor Cured Oak Bourbon - 2015

bottles.desc: Colonel E.H. Taylor, Jr. Cured Oak is a 100-proof, Bottled-in-Bond bourbon aged ten years in barrels built from white oak staves air-dried for 13 months — more than double the standard six-month curing time. For E.H. Taylor devotees, Cured Oak is a deep cut: one of the most coveted bottlings in the lineup, absent for more than a decade. Originally released in 2015 with a 17-year age statement, it became a secondary-market grail commanding $3,999 or more. Its 2026 return arrives as a rare, limited offering rather than an annual release. What sets Cured Oak apart is the wood. Buffalo Traceâ¦
bottles.name: Colonel E.H. Taylor Cured Oak Bourbon - 2026

bottles.desc: Four grains. Distilled in 2005. Aged 12 years. Colonel E.H. Taylor's most unconventional release — and one of the most technically interesting bourbons Buffalo Trace has ever produced. Standard bourbon uses three grains: corn (the majority), a secondary grain (rye or wheat), and malted barley. Colonel E.H. Taylor Four Grain adds a fourth — wheat alongside the corn, rye, and malted barley — creating a mashbill with no precedent in the standard Taylor lineup. Distilled in 2005 at 104 proof and aged 12 years at Buffalo Trace in Frankfort, Kentucky, the result is a bourbon of unusual texturalâ¦
bottles.name: Colonel E.H. Taylor Four Grain Bourbon Whiskey

bottles.desc: 2017 Batch. Four Grain. Bottled-in-Bond. Colonel E.H. Taylor — the 2017 release of the Four Grain expression in Buffalo Trace’s Colonel E.H. Taylor special release program, presenting a four-grain mash bill — corn, rye, wheat, and malted barley — at Bottled-in-Bond standards from one of the most allocated annual release programs in American whiskey. The E.H. Taylor special release program produces a small number of annual limited expressions beyond the core Small Batch and Single Barrel: Four Grain, Amaranth, Seasoned Wood, Cured Oak, and others. The Four Grain is among the most sought-afterâ¦
bottles.name: Colonel E.H. Taylor Four Grain Bourbon Whiskey 2017 Batch

bottles.desc: 2018 Batch. Four Grain. Bottled-in-Bond. Colonel E.H. Taylor — the 2018 release of the Four Grain expression, the consecutive-year companion to the 2017 Four Grain also in the Wooden Cork collection, presenting the same four-grain mash bill at Bottled-in-Bond standards one year later. Where the 2017 Four Grain draws on barrels from the 2017 distillation season, the 2018 batch reflects a different seasonal set of barrels — subtle differences in grain sourcing, fermentation conditions, and barrel placement producing year-over-year variation within the same program template. The four-grain mashâ¦
bottles.name: Colonel E.H. Taylor Four Grain Bourbon Whiskey 2018 Batch

bottles.desc: Colonel E.H. Taylor Seasoned Wood Bourbon is an experimental release from the Colonel E.H. Taylor collection at Buffalo Trace — aged in barrels prepared from oak that was seasoned for an extended period before barrel construction, allowing greater development of the wood’s flavor compounds and producing a uniquely rich, complex bourbon. Colonel E.H. Taylor, Jr. is celebrated as the “Father of the Modern Bourbon Industry.” The seasoned wood approach extends the typical oak preparation cycle, with the staves allowed to mature in open air for longer before barrel construction. This longerâ¦
bottles.name: Colonel E.H. Taylor Seasoned Wood Bourbon Whiskey

bottles.desc: The Colonel E.H. Taylor Small Batch 3-Pack delivers three bottles of the Bottled-in-Bond bourbon that honors Colonel Taylor’s legacy — the most widely distributed expression in the EHT lineup at 100 proof, from century-old warehouses at Buffalo Trace Distillery. E.H. Taylor Small Batch is Bottled in Bond: single distillery, single season, minimum four years aging, 100 proof — the standard Colonel Edmund Haynes Taylor Jr. fought to establish through the Bottled-in-Bond Act of 1897. The 3-pack format provides reliable supply of one of the most consistently popular allocated bourbons in theâ¦

bottles.desc: 134.5 proof. Batch 1. 2012. Colonel E.H. Taylor Barrel Proof — the founding batch of the annual Barrel Proof program, the highest-proof expression across the three in-stock EHT Barrel Proof batches, and from the program’s inaugural year when the concept of a BiB cask strength E.H. Taylor expression was first established. Batch 1 at 134.5 proof is 6.4 proof points above Batch 5 (2016, 127.5) and 5.5 above Batch 11 (2022, 129.0) — the highest-proof of the three current collection EHT Barrel Proof expressions by a significant margin. As the founding batch of a program that has now released overâ¦

bottles.desc: Colonel E.H. Taylor, Jr. Barrel Proof Batch 10 — 127.3 proof, uncut, unfiltered. The tenth release in Buffalo Trace’s annual E.H. Taylor Barrel Proof program, bottled without dilution from the Buffalo Trace Distillery’s Taylor mashbill in honor of Edmund Haynes Taylor Jr., the father of the Bottled-in-Bond Act of 1897. E.H. Taylor, Jr. was instrumental in establishing the standards that define American bourbon today. His lobbying for the Bottled-in-Bond Act — which required single-distillery, single-season production, federally bonded warehouse aging, and bottling at exactly 100 proof —â¦
bottles.name: Colonel E.H. Taylor, Jr. Barrel Proof Batch 10 127.3 Proof 2021

Taylor Barrel Proof — the 2022 annual release, the eleventh batch in the series and one of two Barrel Proof batches in the current Wooden Cork collection alongside Batch 5 (2016, 127.5 proof). The E.H. Taylor Barrel Proof program releases a single annual batch of Bottled-in-Bond cask strength bourbon — meeting both the BiB standard (single distillery, single season, 100 proof minimum, bonded warehouse) and presenting it at natural barrel proof above that threshold. Taylor mash bill at its natural barrel concentration — no dilution, no filtration — under the Bottled-in-Bond framework: single distillery, single distilling season, federally bonded warehouse aging. Taylor Warehouse C is an annual limited Bottled-in-Bond expression from Buffalo Trace, named for and aged in Warehouse C — a historic rickhouse that dates to the Taylor era of the distillery. Taylor Warehouse C Tornado Surviving release: the Tornado Surviving is a one-time bottling of barrels exposed to the 2006 tornado that struck the warehouse; theâ¦

Weller and William Larue Weller programs. A historic grain meeting a historic family recipe. The Weller family bourbon program traces to W.L. Weller's introduction of wheat as the secondary grain in 1849 — replacing rye to produce a softer, sweeter, more caramel-forward bourbon. That recipe became the foundation for Pappy Van Winkle and every Weller expression that followed.â¦

Double Eagle Very Rare — the founding release of Buffalo Trace’s most age-forward and ultra-premium standard program, drawing on the distillery’s deepest aged inventory in a presentation befitting the liquid inside. Double Eagle Very Rare is Buffalo Trace’s apex annual expression — positioned above Eagle Rare 17 Year (BTAC) in the age hierarchy, drawing on 20-year Kentucky straight bourbon from the same distillery that produces Eagle Rare, Buffalo Trace, and the BTAC expressions. First introduced in 2019 and released in tiny annual quantities, Double Eagle Very Rare slumbers two decades on the lower floors of Buffalo Trace's historic warehouses. Each release arrives in a luxurious crystal decanter inside a silver presentation box with anâ¦
bottles.name: Double Eagle Very Rare 20 Year 2025 Straight Bourbon Whiskey

Double Eagle Very Rare — Buffalo Trace Distillery's ultra-premium 20-year-old Kentucky straight bourbon, produced in extraordinarily limited quantities of fewer than 2,000 bottles annually and presented in the hand-blown crystal decanter that makes Double Eagle Very Rare one of the most recognizable ultra-luxury American whiskeys in existence. Double Eagle Very Rare sits above Eagle Rare 17 Year in the Buffalo Trace prestige hierarchy and represents the distillery's most extended age statement in the Eagle Rare program. At 20 years, the bourbon has spent two fullâ¦

Each bottle is drawn from a single barrel selected at Buffalo Trace — meaning each bottle in the pack may show slight barrel-to-barrel variation while sharing the core Eagle Rare profile. One of the most consistently acclaimed value bourbons in America and increasingly difficult toâ¦

Each bottle in the 6-pack is drawn from an individually selected barrel — the consistency of house character combined with the subtle individual variation that single barrel production inherently produces. The toffee, orange peel, herb, honey, leather,â¦

bottles.desc: 101 proof. 10 years. 1978. Quart format. Eagle Rare — the oldest and largest-format founding-era Eagle Rare expression in the Wooden Cork collection, a quart bottle from just three years after the brand’s 1975 creation at Old Prentice Distillery in Lawrenceburg, Kentucky. The 1978 vintage was distilled approximately 1968 — before Eagle Rare even existed as a brand — and bottled in 1978 as a 10-year expression under the newly created label. This places the 1978 Quart at the very earliest production era of the brand, with spirit that predates the Eagle Rare name itself. The quart formatâ¦

Eagle Rare — the largest and rarest format of the founding-era Eagle Rare collection, a 1.75-liter 1978 vintage with original swing stopper from the Old Prentice Distillery in Lawrenceburg, Kentucky. The 1978 vintage already represents the oldest Eagle Rare in the Wooden Cork collection as a standalone quart. The 1.75-liter format with swing stopper is an entirely different tier: over 45 years old, in the largest standard bottle format, with the original swing closure mechanism intact. Half-gallon bottles from this era inâ¦

Eagle Rare 1979 Release — a vintage bottle from the original Eagle Rare formula at the historic 101 proof, predating the Buffalo Trace Antique Collection program by over two decades. Eagle Rare was created in 1975 by Charley Beam at the George T. Stagg Distillery — what is now Buffalo Trace. The original Eagle Rare was bottled at 101 proof (not the 90 proof of the current 10 Year standard expression) and designated as a 10-year bourbon. The 1979 release is from the expression's earliest commercial years — distilled circa 1969 at a distillery thatâ¦

Eagle Rare — a founding-era Eagle Rare from the Old Prentice Distillery in Lawrenceburg, Kentucky, now over 45 years from original bottling and one of three founding-era Eagle Rare expressions in the Wooden Cork collection alongside the 1978 Quart and the 1982 Release. Eagle Rare was created in 1975 at the Old Prentice Distillery (later known as the Four Roses Distillery under Seagram ownership) in Lawrenceburg. The 1970s and early 1980s represent the founding era of the brand — before Buffalo Trace acquired the label and moved production to Frankfort. Theâ¦

Eagle Rare — the 1982 vintage of the founding-era Eagle Rare from Old Prentice Distillery in Lawrenceburg, one year after the 1981 Release also in the Wooden Cork collection, completing a consecutive two-year founding-era pair alongside the 1978 Quart. The 1981 and 1982 releases document two consecutive production years at Old Prentice during the period when Eagle Rare was establishing itself as a premium aged bourbon in the pre-revival American whiskey market. Eagle Rare — the 2000 vintage of Eagle Rare at the 107-proof specification, from the Old Charter-era production under the formula that preceded the current 90-proof 17-year BTAC expression. bottles.desc: Experience the essence of American heritage with Eagle Rare 12 Year Old Kentucky Straight Bourbon Whiskey. This exceptional bourbon embodies the spirit of freedom and innovation, much like the American bald eagle it draws inspiration from. Crafted with precision and aged to perfection, this bourbon offers a taste that is both rich and complex. Tasting Notes Nose: Sweet and oaky, inviting you to explore further. Body: Full and complex, with subtle notes that dance on your palate. Finish: Long-lasting and rich, reminiscent of a fine port wine. Whether you prefer to savor it neat or over ice,â¦

The combination of the expression's 17-year age statement plus nearly 17-18 additional years of bottleâ¦

Eagle Rare 17 Year 2010 — over a decade from its original BTAC fall allocation, from the era when BTAC competition for allocation was increasing but before peak scarcity. Eagle Rare 17 Year 2011 — over a decade from its original BTAC fall allocation, from the era when BTAC competition was intensifying but before the scarcity peak of subsequent years. The 2011 release sits between the deep-vintage 2007 and 2010 expressions and the more recent vintages, representing the mid-decade chapter of the BTAC Eagle Rare 17 Year program. bottles.name: Eagle Rare 17 Year Old Bourbon Whiskey 2011

bottles.desc: 17 years. 90 proof. 2012. Eagle Rare — from the 2012 Buffalo Trace Antique Collection, one of the five annual BTAC expressions and one of the most consistently celebrated allocations in American whiskey. Eagle Rare 17 is the longest-aged standard BTAC expression — at 17 years and 90 proof, it presents a more restrained, elegantly aged profile compared to the higher-proof BTAC expressions (GTS, WLW, THH, Saz18). Where those expressions lead with power and concentration, ER17 leads with refinement: deep dried fruit, leather, and worn oak from 17 years of Kentucky maturation, presented at aâ¦

Eagle Rare 17 Year is one of five annual releases in the Buffalo Trace Antique Collection, alongside George T. Stagg, William Larue Weller, Thomas H. Handy, and Sazerac 18 Year. Of the five, Eagle Rare 17 is the most approachable in both proof and style — 101 proof rather than the 120–130 range of the cask-strength BTAC releases, allowing the 17 years of oak maturation to express itself without the intensity ofâ¦

bottles.desc: After serving in the Louisville Brigade during the Mexican-American war, William Larue Weller returned home to his native Kentucky. His family had helped settle Kentucky in the late 18th century, and his grandfather, Daniel Weller, purchased a distillery in Nelson County in 1800. (When Daniel Weller died, his estate was inventoried and was said to contain three whiskey barrels, two stills, one mash kettle and numerous dry kegs.) Upon his return to Kentucky following the war, W.L. Weller joined the family business and began working at his grandfather's distillery. Weller had a sharp palate andâ¦
bottles.name: W.L. Weller 12 Year Bourbon Gold Foil

bottles.desc: 12+ years. 90 proof. 1.75L. Double Gold at the 2019 San Francisco World Spirits Competition. The wheated bourbon recipe that traces directly to William Larue Weller's 1849 innovation — in the large-format bottle. William Larue Weller returned from the Mexican-American War in the 1840s and joined his family's distilling business. In 1849, he introduced what would become one of bourbon's most significant innovations: a bourbon using wheat as its secondary grain rather than rye. Wheated bourbons — softer, sweeter, and more caramel-forward than rye-recipe bourbons — would go on to define the mostâ¦
bottles.name: W.L. Weller Bourbon 12 Year

bottles.desc: After serving in the Louisville Brigade during the Mexican-American war, William Larue Weller returned home to his native Kentucky. (When Daniel Weller died, his estate was inventoried and was said to contain three whiskey barrels, two stills, one mash kettle and numerous dry kegs.) Upon his return to Kentucky following the war, W.L. Weller joined the family business and began working at his grandfather's distillery. Weller had a sharp palate andâ¦

The Cellar Aged program takes the same wheated bourbon that defines Maker's Mark and extends its maturation in the below-ground cellar environment, where the more stable temperatures and higher humidity of the natural limestone caves produce slower, more gradual spirit-woodâ¦

Created by Bill Samuels, Sr. to be a gentle, easy-drinking bourbon, it's finished with the iconic hand-dipped red wax seal. Bottled at 45% ABV. Enjoy neat, over ice, or in cocktails. The 1.75L is the larger format for entertaining. Tasting Notes Palate: Soft, wheated, caramel-forward; gentle, smooth finish Specs Distillery: Maker's Mark, Loretto, Kentucky Style: Wheated Kentucky Straight Bourbon ABV: 45% (90 Proof) Size: 1.75L Browse the full Maker's Markâ¦

For The Stewards Release, fully matured cask strength bourbon undergoes a secondary maturation with 10 virgin oak staves placed directlyâ¦

Maker's Mark × Kelsey Plum — a limited collaboration between Maker's Mark and WNBA All-Star Kelsey Plum, presenting a Private Selection bourbon built around Plum's bold, uncompromising approach to the game and to everything else. Kelsey Plum is a two-time WNBA champion and one of the most prolific scorers in WNBA history. Her Private Selection philosophy mirrors her on-court identity: push further than expected, trust your instincts, and never settle for the obvious result. The result is Yam Jam — a name as distinctive as the player behind it, and aâ¦
bottles.name: Maker's Mark x Kelsey Plum Yam Jam Private Selection

bottles.desc: Maker's Mark Cellar Aged 2024 is a limited annual release of the distillery's wheated Kentucky Straight Bourbon — a blend of 12- and 13-year-old barrels given extended maturation in the natural limestone cellar beneath Star Hill Farm, bottled at 119.3 proof. The Cellar Aged program finishes mature Maker's Mark in the below-ground limestone cellar in Loretto, Kentucky, where stable, cooler temperatures slow spirit-wood interaction and develop additional complexity that the standard above-ground rick houses cannot replicate. The 2024 edition draws on unusually old stock for Maker's — 12 and 13â¦
bottles.name: Maker’s Mark Cellar Aged 2024 Straight Bourbon

bottles.desc: Experience the Spirit of Champions Introducing the Maker’s Mark Champion Selection Hand Picked by LA Dodgers Bourbon , a unique blend crafted for those who appreciate the finer things in life. This exclusive bourbon is a celebration of excellence, combining the rich heritage of Maker’s Mark with the legendary spirit of the LA Dodgers. Perfect for sports enthusiasts and bourbon lovers alike, this bottle is a must-have for any collection. Tasting Notes Aroma: A delightful mix of vanilla and caramel with a hint of oak. This special release is a tribute to the dedication and passion shared by both Maker’s Mark and the Dodgers. This attention to detail ensures a bourbon that is aged to taste, not time, offering aâ¦
bottles.name: Maker’s Mark LA Dodgers 2025 Limited Edition Bourbon

bottles.desc: Crafted Heritage: The Making of Maker’s Mark Lost Recipe Series: Edition 2 2025 Release Archived Stave: Mocha The Maker’s Mark Lost Recipe Series: Edition 2 2025 Release Archived Stave: Mocha represents a thoughtfully revived experiment from a house renowned for its craftsmanship and stubborn pursuit of flavor nuance. This limited edition borrows the Mocha stave—an archival exploration that coaxed deep chocolate and coffee notes from the wood—then reintroduces it to a modern bourbon profile. The result is a premium bourbon experience that marries tradition with audacious flavor, offering aâ¦
bottles.name: Maker’s Mark Lost Recipe Series: Edition 2 2025 Release Archived Stave: Mocha

bottles.desc: Maker's Mark USA 250th Anniversary Limited Edition is a commemorative bottling of classic Maker's Mark Kentucky Straight Bourbon Whisky released to mark the United States' 250th anniversary in 2026 — the same wheated bourbon at 90 proof in a limited patriotic bottle featuring red, white, and blue wax detailing, with a portion of sales supporting the Farmer Veteran Coalition's programs for veterans transitioning into agricultural careers. PRE-ORDER ITEM | EXPECTED TO SHIP END OF JUNE 2026 Maker's Mark has been produced at Star Hill Farm in Loretto, Kentucky since 1954 using a wheated mash billâ¦
bottles.name: Maker’s Mark USA 250th Anniversary Bourbon Limited Edition

bottles.desc: Both Bomberger's and Shenk's have been released once again by Michter's to honor John Shenk, the founder of the original Bomberger Distillery (later known as Michter's Distillery). If you haven't heard of Bomberger's or Shenk's, you are missing out on some fantastic bourbon! New editions of the hidden gems from Michter's and yes they put their name on them this time. Great new bottle with hand numbered labels. Bomberger's stepping it up from the previous 100 Proof to a nice 108 and Shenk's up from 80 Proof to 91.2 - both adding richness and a heavier mouth coating with these releases. Nose:â¦

bottles.desc: Bomberger's Declaration Bourbon. Michter's Distillery, Louisville, Kentucky — a robust Kentucky Straight Bourbon Whiskey bottled at 108 proof, honoring the legacy of the original Bomberger's Distillery in Schaefferstown, Pennsylvania — which operated in the 1800s, became known as Michter's in the 20th century, and is recognized as America's first whiskey distillery. Each annual vintage is released under the same production standards as the broader Michter's program. Bomberger's Declaration takes its name and identity from the original distillery that John Shenk founded in 1753, which laterâ¦
bottles.name: Bomberger's Declaration 2021 Bourbon

bottles.desc: Bomberger’s Declaration Distillery Bourbon is a robust Kentucky Straight Bourbon Whiskey, bottled at 108 proof with a broad spectrum of distinctively rich characters. It is matured in special barrels, some of which were made from Chinquapin oak that has been naturally air dried for over 3 years. The distillery honors the legacy of the former distillery known as Bomberger’s in the 1800s and later as Michter’s in the 20th Century.
bottles.name: Bomberger's Declaration 2023 Bourbon

bottles.desc: Bomberger's Declaration 2024 Bourbon. Michter's Distillery, Louisville, Kentucky — the most recent vintage of the 108 proof Kentucky Straight Bourbon Whiskey honoring the legacy of the Bomberger's Distillery in Schaefferstown, Pennsylvania — which operated in the 1800s before becoming known as Michter's in the 20th century, the modern operation of America's first whiskey distillery (est. 1753 as Shenk's). The 2024 is the most recent Bomberger's Declaration vintage, produced alongside Shenk's Homestead 2024 as Michter's annual tribute to both pillars of the Shenk/Bomberger's foundingâ¦
bottles.name: Bomberger's Declaration 2024 Bourbon

bottles.desc: Bomberger's Declaration 2026 Release is a limited annual Kentucky Straight Bourbon from Michter's Legacy Series — aged with specially toasted and charred Chinquapin oak alongside select Hungarian oak, bottled at 108 proof. An eagerly awaited annual release from Michter's, the 2026 Bomberger's Declaration leans on barrel innovation: specially toasted and charred Chinquapin oak combined with select Hungarian oak for added depth and complexity. Bottled at a robust 108 proof, it delivers a bold yet refined Kentucky Straight Bourbon that collectors seek out each year. Tasting Notes Nose: Darkâ¦
bottles.name: Bomberger's Declaration 2026 Release

bottles.desc: Bomberger’s Declaration 2025 Release | Kentucky Straight Bourbon – 108 Proof The 2025 Bomberger’s Declaration is a bold continuation of Michter’s Legacy Series, crafted in honor of the historic Bomberger’s Distillery. This limited-edition Kentucky straight bourbon is aged in a combination of traditional and Chinquapin oak barrels , seasoned for up to five years before toasting and charring. Bottled at a potent 108 proof , it offers intense complexity, robust mouthfeel, and a beautifully layered finish. With its rich structure and signature spice, this 2025 release is a masterclass in barrelâ¦
bottles.name: Bomberger’s Declaration 2025 Release Kentucky Straight Bourbon Whiskey

bottles.desc: This 2022 release continues the Bomberger’s tradition of offering bold and beautiful 108 proof Kentucky Straight Bourbon with a rich and layered complexity due to a portion of the bourbon being aged in Chinquapin (Quercus muehlenbergii) oak. The Chinquapin oak utilized for aging was naturally air dried and seasoned for three years before being toasted and charred to exacting specifications.
bottles.name: Bomberger’s Declaration Bourbon 2022

bottles.desc: Bomberger's PFG™ (Precision Fine Grain) Kentucky Straight Bourbon. Michter's Distillery, Louisville, Kentucky — a groundbreaking expression that applies Precision Fine Grain™ technology to the Bomberger's program: fully matured in new toasted and charred American oak barrels, then finished in custom French fine grain oak barrels crafted from wood air-dried for over 40 months in France from the Tronçais, Allier, Nevers, and Vosges forests, bottled at 100.2 proof (50.1% ABV) — mahogany red in color with burnt sugar, spice, and chocolate on the nose, and a full-bodied, creamy palate of fruit,â¦
bottles.name: Bomberger’s PFG™ (Precision Fine Grain) Bourbon

bottles.desc: Single barrel. Uncut. Barrel strength. The Fort Nelson Reserve — Michter's distillery-exclusive expression from their flagship Fort Nelson distillery in downtown Louisville, approved by Master Distiller Dan McKee. Michter's Fort Nelson Distillery opened in 2019 in the historic Fort Nelson Building on Whiskey Row in downtown Louisville — a restored 1880s structure at the heart of Louisville's bourbon revival. The Fort Nelson Reserve is Michter's barrel-strength expression available through the distillery's own programs: single barrels selected and approved by Master Distiller Dan McKee,â¦
bottles.name: Michter's 'Fort Nelson Reserve' Barrel Strength Bourbon

Michter's — a named collector account private barrel selection of Michter's 10-year bourbon, the most exclusively sourced presentation of the 10-year expression in the current Wooden Cork catalog. Michter's 10-year bourbon is already among the most limited annual expressions in the distillery's lineup — released only when Andrea Wilson determines available barrels meet the standard. A private barrel selection adds a second layer of exclusivity: rather than the standard 10-year bottling, this is a specific individual barrel chosen by theâ¦

Michter's 10 Year is a single barrel release at a non-disclosed barrel strength, selected by Michter's master distiller from barrels that have reached the 10-year mark and are deemed ready — not released on a fixed calendar. Carefully matured for two full decades and bottled at natural barrel strength, this historic release showcases the extraordinary depth, richness, and balance that have made Michter’s 20 Year one of the most respectedâ¦
bottles.name: Michter's 20 Year Old Bourbon 2014 Single Barrel #H511

Michter's 20 Year is released in very small quantities — typically fewer than 1,000 cases annually — and each batch is hand-selected by master distiller Dan McKee only when barrels have reached the precise characterâ¦

Michter's — the 2021 release of Michter's ultra-selective 20-year bourbon program, the oldest of the four Michter's 20-year expressions in the current Wooden Cork collection alongside the 2022 H2518, 2022 H2515, and 2024 I3209 releases. The 2021 Batch I2612 is the earliest-vintage 20-year in the current four-release collection, predating the other three by one to three years andâ¦

Michter’s 20 Year is produced in extremely limited annual batches from barrels selected by Master Distiller Pamela Heilmann and Master of Maturation Andrea Wilson for optimal peak maturation. Michter's — one of the rarest annual American whiskey releases produced by any distillery, the 2022 release of Michter's most prestigious expression, aged two full decades at the Fort Nelson Distillery in Louisville. Years can pass without a Michter's 20-year release when the available stock doesn't meet the standard. The 2022 H2518 batch represents a specificâ¦

Michter's — the 2024 release of Michter's ultra-selective 20-year bourbon program, one of three consecutive release years in the current Wooden Cork collection alongside the 2022 H2518 and the 2025 Edition. Michter's — the most current of the four Michter's 20-year bourbon releases in the Wooden Cork collection, following the 2018 I372, 2021 I2612, and 2022 H2518 and H2515 expressions. The Michter's 25-year is released only in years when Andrea Wilson determines that available stock has reached the exceptional quality threshold — the same quality-over-calendar philosophy that governs the 20-year and 10-year programs, but applied to the most aged and finite inventory. Historically, the 25-year has beenâ¦

bottles.desc: The rarest single-age expression in the Michter's production program. Michter's 25 Year Old Bourbon — released only when Master Distiller Dan McKee and Master of Maturation Andrea Wilson jointly determine that available 25-year barrels have achieved the extraordinary standard the expression demands. Years pass between releases. The 2023 is among the most precious approved vintages in recent memory. Michter's releases the 25 Year only when specific barrels of extraordinary quality have reached 25 years of maturation and both McKee and Wilson agree the liquid is ready. There is no fixedâ¦
bottles.name: Michter's 25 Year Old Bourbon 2023

bottles.desc: Michter's US*1 Small Batch Kentucky Straight Bourbon. Michter's Fort Nelson Distillery, Louisville, Kentucky — the flagship everyday expression of America's first whiskey company, distilled from a sour mash of select grains, barrel entry-proofed well below the legal maximum at approximately 103 proof to preserve grain character, aged in heat-cycled warehouses that extend bourbon expansion beyond natural seasons, and bottled at 45.7% ABV — camphor and concentrated sweetness on the nose with corn, wood smoke, and rye underpinning, and a long finish of wood and sweet maple syrup. Michter'sâ¦

bottles.desc: 10 years. Single barrel. The 2014 annual release of Michter's 10 Year Single Barrel Bourbon — now over a decade from its original release date, available only through specialty retailers with legacy inventory. Michter's 10 Year Single Barrel is the distillery's annual limited expression — released each year when Master Distiller Dan McKee (in consultation with Master of Maturation Andrea Wilson) determines that available 10-year barrels have reached peak quality. Unlike mass-produced 10-year bourbons that bottle on a fixed schedule, Michter's releases the 10 Year only when specific barrelsâ¦
bottles.name: Michter's Single Barrel 10 Year Old Bourbon 2014

bottles.desc: Single barrel. 10 years of patient aging. One of the rarest surviving Michter's 10 Year vintages — named "Best American Whiskey" by Food & Wine magazine. The 2015 vintage is now a decade out of primary distribution — found only through specialty retailers with legacy inventory. For collectors assembling a Michter's vertical, the 2014 and 2015 vintages are the hardest years to source. The critical acclaim is consistent across vintages: Food & Wine namedâ¦
bottles.name: Michter's Single Barrel 10 Year Old Bourbon 2015

The 2016 vintage represents a specific cohort of barrels from that year's approval, each bottled individually. As an older vintage now out of primary distribution, the 2016 is found only through specialty retailers with legacy inventory. At 10â¦
bottles.name: Michter's Single Barrel 10 Year Old Bourbon 2016

bottles.desc: Single barrel. 10 years of patient aging. Named "Best American Whiskey" by Food & Wine magazine. The 2017 vintage of Michter's most decorated expression. Some years produce no release at all. The 2017 vintage is a specific selection of barrels from that year's approval, bottled individually as single barrels, meaning no two bottles from different barrels are identical. Michter's is based inâ¦
bottles.name: Michter's Single Barrel 10 Year Old Bourbon 2017

bottles.desc: Single barrel. 10 years of patient aging. Named "Best American Whiskey" by Food & Wine magazine. The 2018 vintage of Michter's most decorated expression. Michter's 10 Year Single Barrel is released only when Master Distiller and Master of Maturation determine that available barrels have peaked — a standard that means some years see no release at all. The 2018 vintage represents a specific cohort of barrels approved that year, each bottled individually as single barrels. The critical acclaim for the Michter's 10 Year is consistent across vintages: Food & Wine named it the Best Americanâ¦
bottles.name: Michter's Single Barrel 10 Year Old Bourbon 2018

bottles.desc: Single barrel. 10 years of patient aging. Named "Best American Whiskey" by Food & Wine magazine. The 2019 vintage of Michter's most celebrated expression. Michter's 10 Year Single Barrel is released only when Master Distiller and Master of Maturation determine that available barrels have peaked. No commercial calendar drives the release — only barrel quality. The 2019 vintage is a specific cohort of barrels from that year's approval, each bottled individually as single barrels. It is more recent than the 2015–2017 vintages but equally constrained in supply. At 10 years, Michter's bourbon hasâ¦
bottles.name: Michter's Single Barrel 10 Year Old Bourbon 2019

bottles.desc: 10 years. Single Barrel. 2020 Release. Michter's — Michter's most decorated expression, awarded Best American Whiskey by multiple independent judges, presenting a single barrel Kentucky straight bourbon selected from heat-cycled Michter's warehouses for the distinctive depth and complexity that 10 years of controlled maturation produces. Michter's uses a heat-cycling process in its warehouses — artificially warming the warehouses in late fall to extend the period of spirit expansion into the wood, then allowing natural cooling. This additional expansion cycle extracts more flavor compoundsâ¦

bottles.desc: Michter's Single Barrel 10 Year Old Bourbon 2021 is the latest vintage of the most decorated expression in the Michter's portfolio — named Best American Whiskey by Food & Wine magazine, produced in limited quantities from single barrels that have met Michter's exacting quality standard after a minimum of ten years of aging in heat-cycled warehouses with the low barrel entry proof that defines the Michter's production philosophy. Michter's releases the 10 Year Single Barrel Bourbon only when barrels that have reached a decade of age are deemed exceptional enough to meet the standard — not on aâ¦
bottles.name: Michter's Single Barrel 10 Year Old Bourbon 2021

bottles.desc: Michter's Single Barrel 10 Year Old Bourbon 2023. Michter's Fort Nelson Distillery, Louisville, Kentucky — single barrel, 10 years of patient aging, named "Best American Whiskey" by Food & Wine magazine, released only when Master Distiller Dan McKee and Master of Maturation Andrea Wilson determine available barrels have reached peak maturity. Michter's 10 Year is not produced on a commercial calendar. McKee and Wilson evaluate available 10-year barrels on merit alone — in years when no barrels meet the standard, no release occurs. The 2023 vintage represents a specific cohort of barrelsâ¦
bottles.name: Michter's Single Barrel 10 Year Old Bourbon 2023

bottles.desc: Michter's Single Barrel 10 Year Old Bourbon 2024. Michter's Fort Nelson Distillery, Louisville, Kentucky — single barrel, 10 years of patient aging, named "Best American Whiskey" by Food & Wine magazine, released only when Master Distiller Dan McKee and Master of Maturation Andrea Wilson determine available barrels have reached peak maturity. The 2024 vintage joins a short list of approved annual releases from Michter's contingent 10 Year program. McKee and Wilson evaluate available 10-year barrels on merit — in years when no barrels meet the standard, no release occurs. Each bottle is aâ¦
bottles.name: Michter's Single Barrel 10 Year Old Bourbon 2024

bottles.desc: Michter's Single Barrel 10 Year Old Bourbon 2025. Michter's Fort Nelson Distillery, Louisville, Kentucky — single barrel, 10 years of patient aging, named "Best American Whiskey" by Food & Wine magazine, released only when Master Distiller Dan McKee and Master of Maturation Andrea Wilson determine available barrels have reached peak maturity. The most recent vintage in Wooden Cork's Michter's 10 Year collection. The 2025 vintage is the newest approved release in Michter's contingent 10 Year program and the most current expression of what Michter's heat-cycled warehouse maturation, low barrelâ¦
bottles.name: Michter's Single Barrel 10 Year Old Bourbon 2025

bottles.desc: Mature in age and truly exceptional in quality, this bourbon has earned its place as an enduring favorite of the most discerning whiskey connoisseurs. The "Best American Whiskey" according to Food & Wine magazine, Michters 10 Year represents the culmination of patient aging. Indulge in the timeless perfection of Michters 10 Year Bourbon. This award-winning spirit has been deemed the "Best American Whiskey" by reputable experts in the industry, such as Food & Wine magazine. With an incomparable level of maturity and unparalleled quality, it's easy to see why this bourbon is a top choice amongâ¦
bottles.name: Michter's Single Barrel 10 Year Old Bourbon 2026

bottles.desc: Michter's US-1 Toasted Barrel Finish Bourbon Whiskey. Michter's Fort Nelson Distillery, Louisville, Kentucky — a limited release that begins with fully matured barrels of Michter's US*1 Small Batch Kentucky Straight Bourbon, then transfers the whiskey into a second barrel made from 18-month air-dried wood that has been toasted to precise specifications but not charred — adding a specific layer of toasted wood sweetness, caramelized grain character, and vanilla depth that the standard US*1 Bourbon does not have. Michter's Toasted Barrel expressions represent the distillery's most experimentalâ¦
bottles.name: Michter's US-1 Toasted Barrel Finish Bourbon Whiskey

bottles.desc: The founding First Release. Michter's US-1 Toasted Barrel Finish Bourbon 2014 — the inaugural edition of one of American whiskey's most innovative annual programs: finishing Kentucky straight bourbon in a second barrel whose interior has been toasted (not charred) to draw out specific caramelized wood sugars unavailable from standard charred barrels. Toasting and charring are fundamentally different processes. Standard bourbon aging uses charred new oak barrels — the char creates a layer of activated carbon and caramelizes the outermost wood sugars. Michter's Toasted Barrel process uses aâ¦
bottles.name: Michter's US-1 Toasted Barrel Finish Bourbon Whiskey 2014 First Release

bottles.desc: The Second Release. Michter's US-1 Toasted Barrel Finish Bourbon 2015 — the second annual edition of Michter's toasted barrel finish program, following the founding 2014 First Release in the Wooden Cork collection.
